sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports pain in the inside of his right knee, describing it as sharp and worsening with flexion. He reports the pain started yesterday after a fall while changing a light bulb on a ladder. He has been taking ibuprofen with minimal relief. He also reports pain in the left hand.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals tenderness to palpation over the medial aspect of the right knee, particularly with valgus stress. Sensation is intact. The x-ray shows no fractures or dislocations, but some fluid is present.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The clinician suspects a medial meniscus sprain and notes tenderness over the medial meniscus area. The patient's history includes prior afib and a previous nose job.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Order a knee brace and crutches for temporary use. Recommend motrin 800 mg every 6 hours as needed for pain. Schedule a follow-up appointment in one week to assess progress and consider an MRI if symptoms do not improve.
